channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Opposition to the Arusha Peace Accord Contributed to the 1994 Genocide in Rwanda
item.description: The article explains how opposition to the Arusha Peace Accord of August 1993 in Rwanda, mainly by Hutu extremists, helped set the stage for the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi. While most Rwandans we...

item.title: Post Office engages £16.3m PR firm amid ongoing legal disputes with scandal victims
item.description: The Post Office has reportedly entered into a £16.324 million contract with a public relations firm to manage the fallout from the long-running scandal surrounding its Horizon IT system. This decision...

item.title: IMD Issues Orange Alert as Strong Winds and Dust Storm Affect Delhi-NCR
item.description: On 15th May 2026, the Delhi-NCR region experienced severe weather conditions as strong winds and a dust storm swept across the area. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued an orange alert fo...

item.title: Iran Accuses UAE of Supporting US-Israeli Military Actions
item.description: Iran has publicly accused the United Arab Emirates (UAE) of actively participating in military operations led by the United States and Israel. Tehran’s statement highlights growing tensions in the Mid...

item.title: Profile of U.S. Farmers Market Shoppers: Trends and Motivations
item.description: A study of U.S. consumers reveals that people who shop at the more than 8,700 farmers markets across the country generally fall into six distinct groups. Three groups—the highly engaged, health-focuse...

item.title: Riverton man charged with second-degree murder after missing man’s remains found in vehicle
item.description: RCMP officers from the Gimli detachment executed a search warrant on a rural property in Riverton, Manitoba, where they discovered the remains of 39-year-old Darryl Lambert in a vehicle. Lambert had b...

item.title: Bedridden woman dies in house fire amid Caloocan blackout
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Caloocan City when a bedridden woman lost her life in a residential fire that coincided with a citywide brownout. According to reports, the fire broke out in Barangay 145...

item.title: Understanding What Shapes Nigerian Beliefs and Values
item.description: Na fact sey religion dey waka hand-in-hand with everyday life for Nigeria no be small thing. Nigerians strongly believe in God, whether na Allah, Olodumare, or the Father, and this dey influence how d...

item.title: Brian Sigu and Kidum Survive Road Accident, Rushed to Karen Hospital
item.description: Veteran Kenyan musician Brian Sigu was involved in a serious road accident along with fellow artist Kidum, leaving fans concerned across social media. The two were reportedly travelling in a vintage R...

item.title: Experimental smart contact lenses show potential in altering depression-like behaviour in mice
item.description: Researchers in South Korea have developed experimental contact lenses designed to deliver mild electrical signals through the retina to brain regions associated with mood. The study, conducted on mice...

item.title: Betty Hutton’s Early Modeling Work for Bonwit Teller in 1940
item.description: In December 1940, a young Betty Hutton, aged 19, appeared in LOOK Magazine modeling an ice skating costume for the luxury Fifth Avenue department store, Bonwit Teller. At the time, Hutton was gaining ...

item.title: The Economic and Environmental Value of Bats in U.S. Agriculture
item.description: Bats play a crucial role in the U.S. economy and ecosystem by eating insects, pollinating plants, and providing natural fertilizer through their guano. Yet, bat populations are rapidly declining due t...

item.title: Why the World Needs a Break from Music Biopics
item.description: Rebecca Shaw argues that the proliferation of musical biopics has become exhausting and repetitive. She references the recent Michael Jackson film, which achieved box office success despite negative r...

item.title: California Lawmakers Raise Concerns About Federal Support and Immigration Issues Ahead of 2028 Olympics
item.description: As Los Angeles prepares to host the 2028 Olympics, state lawmakers are expressing concerns about potential complications stemming from tensions with the Trump administration. Key issues include the se...

item.title: The Art and Legacy of Bernard Blossac in 20th Century Fashion Illustration
item.description: Bernard Blossac (1917–2004) was a prominent French fashion illustrator known for his sophisticated and graceful depictions of haute couture. His work, primarily executed in ink and watercolor, showcas...

item.title: Rwanda to Play Friendly Matches Against Tanzania and Comoros in Morocco
item.description: Rwanda's football federation has announced plans to play friendly matches against Tanzania and Comoros during the June FIFA international window. According to Rwanda FA Secretary General Bonnie Mugabe...

item.title: Blast at Madhya Pradesh firecracker factory kills five, injures 23
item.description: A tragic incident occurred at a firecracker factory in Madhya Pradesh, where an unexpected explosion resulted in the death of five workers and injuries to twenty-three others. The explosion took place...

item.title: WBC president discusses returning annual convention and national boxing tournament to South Africa
item.description: World Boxing Council (WBC) president Mauricio Sulaiman has indicated that he is exploring the possibility of holding the WBC annual convention in South Africa once more, nearly three decades after the...

item.title: Tanzania Expands Dar es Salaam Port Oil Storage to Improve Fuel Handling and Efficiency
item.description: Tanzania has kicked off a major project worth TZS 701.8 billion to construct 15 new oil storage tanks at Dar es Salaam Port, aimed at improving national energy security and port efficiency. The expans...

item.title: A Contemporary Translation of the Isa Upanishad Explaining the Nature of the Self
item.description: This article presents an excerpt from the Isa Upanishad, translated from Sanskrit by Nachiketa Jha, offering insights for modern readers. The Upanishad describes the self as all-pervasive, beyond the ...
